title: EB-5 Visa Complete Guide: Investment Criteria, Timelines, and FAQs
content:
## EB-5 Visa Complete Guide (2026): Investment Criteria, Timelines, and FAQs for Investors
- What is the EB-5 Visa?
- What are the Benefits?
- EB-5 Investment Options
- The Different EB-5 Visa Categories
- Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide
- How Long Does the EB-5 Process Take?
- Latest News and Policy Changes

## What is the EB-5 Visa?
The EB-5 visa provides a path to U.S. permanent residency through investment. Foreign investors, who invest $800,000 to $1,050,000 in a qualifying U.S. business and create or preserve 10 full-time U.S. jobs for qualifying workers, can obtain conditional green cards for themselves, their spouses, and unmarried children under 21.
The EB-5 Immigrant Investor Program offers two investment routes: direct investment in a commercial enterprise or investment through USCIS-approved Regional Centers. The minimum investment is $800,000 in rural or high-unemployment areas (targeted employment areas and certain qualifying infrastructure projects) and $1,050,000 elsewhere.
At Colombo & Hurd, we’ve helped clients from over 100 countries navigate the EB-5 process and maintain a 100% approval rate in EB-5 visa cases. The key is understanding which investment route aligns with your goals and timeline.
The EB-5 program was updated in 2022 with the EB-5 Reform and Integrity Act (RIA). The RIA, signed into law on March 15, 2022, introduced visa set-aside categories designed to improve visa availability for certain investments , and introduced concurrent filing and an Adjustment of Status application for permanent residency, allowing eligible investors already in the U.S. (with an immigrant visa number available) to file both steps simultaneously and apply for work authorization while the adjustment application is pending.

## What are the benefits of an EB-5 Visa?
Pursuing an EB-5 visa offers numerous advantages relative to other potential green card options. Benefits of pursuing your green card through the EB-5 program include:
- Permanent residency : Obtain a green card for yourself, spouse, and unmarried children under 21
- Path to citizenship : Eligible for U.S. citizenship after 5 years of residency
- Work authorization : Spouses and children can work in the U.S.
- Education : Access to U.S. education system
- Travel flexibility : Travel freely in and out of the U.S.
- Tax benefits : Potential tax advantages, including reduced tax rates, no tax on foreign assets, estate tax exemptions, and tax credits
- Concurrent filing : Investors can concurrently file I-526, I-485, I-131, and Form I-765
- “Current” visa status : EB-5 is one of few visa categories that currently has green cards available, meaning there is no visa backlog in this category, application processing is faster, and visas are available for immediate issuance.
Most importantly, the EB-5 visa provides a direct pathway to U.S. citizenship and permanent residency, a benefit unmatched by temporary worker visas and E-2 Treaty Investor visas. The pathway to citizenship via the EB-5 visa program is well-documented and fully within the scope of the program.

## EB-5 Investment Options: Direct Investment vs. Regional Centers
The EB-5 program offers two primary investment routes: Direct Investment and Regional Centers. Direct Investment involves investing directly in a U.S. business that will directly employ 10 full-time positions. The Regional Center route involves investing in a USCIS-approved Regional Center, which pools funds from multiple investors to finance projects. Based on recent post-RIA adjudication trends, many investors have experienced faster processing times through the Regional Center route compared to Direct Investment, though timelines vary and are not guaranteed.
If you invest in a TEA (a rural area or area with unemployment at least 150% of the national average), the minimum investment amount is lower regardless of whether you choose Direct Investment or a Regional Center.
Understanding the differences between these options helps you make an informed decision about which route fits your goals.
Direct control over business operations
Limited control; managed by regional center
$800,000 (TEA) or $1,050,000 (standard)
$800,000 (TEA) or $1,050,000 (standard)
Must create 10 direct, full-time U.S. jobs for qualifying employees within the enterprise receiving the investment; investor personally responsible
Regional center structures projects and provides economic modeling to demonstrate job creation; may count direct, indirect, and induced jobs
Investor generates and maintains all documentation to demonstrate job creation, capital deployment, and ongoing compliance
Regional center provides documentation support for project-level and job-creation evidence, though the investor remains responsible for the accuracy of their petition
Active involvement in management or policy formation required
Passive investment; minimal involvement
Lower fees; generally no regional center administrative fees
Higher fees including administrative and management fees
Higher individual project risk
Risk depends on the specific project; capital may be pooled with other investors but is not guaranteed to be diversified
Pooled direct investments are generally not permitted post-RIAl each investor must be credited with their own job creation
Multiple investors can pool funds

### Benefits of Direct Investment
Direct Investment provides more control over business operations. You generally have more flexibility for choosing the investment type and location. Sometimes you have the opportunity for higher potential returns. You maintain a clear understanding of all financial transactions, as you are personally responsible for all transactions.

### Challenges of Direct Investment
Direct Investment requires hands-on management. You are personally responsible for creating 10 jobs. To demonstrate job creation, direct investors rely on evidence like W-2s, I-9s , payroll records, and other employment documentation. You are required to generate, maintain, and provide the necessary documentation to demonstrate compliance with deployment and job creation requirements. Pooled direct investments are no longer allowed; only one investor is allowed per business.

### Benefits of Regional Center Investment
Regional Center investment allows for more hands-off investment management. Regional centers structure projects and provide economic and project documentation to support the job creation requirements for investors. There are more avenues available for regional centers to demonstrate the requisite number of jobs are created. Regional centers can use Input-Output models, providing more opportunities for capturing job creation. Your investments are spread across the project’s capital structure (and may be part of a pooled EB-5 offering). You benefit from the regional center’s industry knowledge. The documentation process is often simplified.

### Challenges of Regional Center Investment
Additional administrative and management fees apply. You have less control over investment decisions. The success of your investment depends on the regional center’s performance. Your potential returns may be lower due to administrative, management, and marketing fees, and project-level costs. Regional centers are subject to USCIS scrutiny, including recordkeeping requirements, site visits, audits, annual statements, and filing requirements, with the aim of protecting EB-5 investors.Our team at Colombo & Hurd offers comprehensive guidance on business immigration , helping you navigate complexities and avoid potential legal pitfalls. We help you choose the right option based on your investment goals, risk tolerance, and management preferences.

## Who qualifies for an EB-5 visa?
Investors for the EB-5 visa must meet specific requirements, which include the following investment requirements :
- Minimum investment requirement: $1,050,000 (standard) or $800,000 in a TEA
- Investing in a new commercial, for-profit enterprise
- Investing capital in the form of cash, assets, or indebtedness (secured by assets)
Applicants must also meet job creation requirements, including:
- Creating or preserving 10 permanent, full-time jobs for qualified U.S. workers
- The job types can be direct employees, indirect employees, or induced employees
- The jobs must last at least 2 years
Alongside these investment and job creation requirements, to be eligible for the EB-5 visa, applicants must be of good moral character, having no felony or misdemeanor convictions, pass a thorough background check, be able to maintain health insurance for dependents, and comply with U.S. tax laws.
There are also certain documentation requirements for those applying for the EB-5 visa:
- Form I-526: Immigrant Petition by Standalone Investor
- Business plan: Applicants must provide a comprehensive business plan
- Financial statements and tax returns: Applicants must provide their personal and business financial statements and tax returns
- Proof of investment: Applicants must provide evidence of their investment
Consult an immigration attorney at Colombo & Hurd to ensure compliance with EB-5 program requirements and regulations.

## Do you meet EB-5 requirements?
Check your EB-5 visa eligibility.

## What are the different EB-5 Visa Categories?
EB-5 visa requirements and qualifications vary depending on the visa class: Unreserved, Rural Set Aside, High Unemployment Set Aside, and Infrastructure Set Aside. The set aside visas are also called reserved visas. These categories were established when the RIA was enacted on March 15, 2022.
No specific quota (receives the remaining percentages EB-5 visas after set-asides are allocated)
Investments that do not qualify for any set-aside category, including many urban projects; legacy petitions filed before March 15, 2022 are also unreserved
Population under 20,000; not in/adjacent to metropolitan statistical area (MSA)
From March 15, 2022 to September 30, 2026
High Unemployment Set Aside
Unemployment rate ≥150% of national average
From March 15, 2022 to September 30, 2026
Infrastructure Set Aside
Infrastructure projects administered by a federal, state, or local- government entity
From March 15, 2022 to September 30, 2026
The Unreserved category comprises all EB-5 visas not specifically reserved in the Rural, High Unemployment, and Infrastructure Set Aside categories. This category does not have a specific quota. Visa availability is greater under this category. Qualification for this category is automatic if you filed your I-526 petition before March 15, 2022. You are also eligible for this category if your investment does not meet the criteria for the visa set-aside categories.
The Set Aside categories are exclusively available to applicants who filed I-526 or I-526E (Immigrant Petition by Regional Center Investor) petitions on or after March 15, 2022. The EB-5 program allocates set-asides for Rural projects (20% of annual EB-5 visas), High Unemployment projects (10%), and Infrastructure projects (2%), each requiring a minimum investment of $800,000. After September 30, 2022, new policies may change these allocations.
An EB-5 visa is considered Rural if the investment project is in an area with a population less than 20,000. The area must not be within a MSA or adjacent to an MSA. An investment qualifies as High Unemployment if it is in an area where the unemployment rate is at least 150% of the national average unemployment rate. EB-5 Infrastructure projects involve government-administered capital investments in public works, funded through regional centers or new commercial enterprises.
Unused set-aside visas are carried over within their respective categories for the next fiscal year. Any remaining unused visas in these categories after the second fiscal year are then allocated to the unreserved categories in the third fiscal year.

## The EB-5 Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide
The EB-5 visa application process is complex and technical. Certain events must be timed to correspond with certain legal filings. In general, the process works as follows:

### Step 1: Pre-Filing Preparation
- Consult an immigration attorney for guidance on the process and to proceed with legal filing
- Choose your investment route (either direct or via regional center)
- Select your investment project (your attorney cannot select the project for you)
- Document the lawful source and transfer of investment funds.

### Step 2: File Form I-526 (Immigrant Petition by Standalone Investor) or I-526E (Immigrant Petition by Regional Center Investor)
- File Form I-526 (Immigrant Petition by Standalone Investor) or Form I-526E (Immigrant Petition by Regional Center Investor).
- Submit business plan, financial statements, and tax returns
- Pay USCIS filing fee (currently $3,675 for I-526 and $4,675 for I-526E, which includes a $3,675 base fee plus $1,000 Integrity Fund fee, and $3,750 for I-829 as of November 2025. Note: Fee changes are proposed for early 2026. Check current USCIS filing fees )

### Step 3: USCIS Review.
- Review business plan and investment
- Conduct a background check

### Step 4: Conditional Residency.
Upon passing USCIS review:
- You will receive a 2-year conditional green card (temporary 2-year green card status)
- You can now enter and legally live in the United States

### Step 5: File Form I-829
File Form I-829 (Petition by Investor to Remove Conditions on Permanent Resident Status).
- Submit evidence of job creation and investment
- Pay filing fee ($3,750 as of November 2025)

### Step 6: USCIS Review and Approval.
- Verify job creation and investment

### Step 7: Permanent Residency.
Upon USCIS approval, you will:
- Receive unconditional green card
- Obtain permanent residency

## How Long does the EB-5 process Take?
Processing times for EB-5 applications and approvals vary significantly and are subject to change based on USCIS workload, visa availability, and individual case factors.
Based on industry experience and recent post–RIA adjudication trends, particularly over the last year, many practitioners have observed a noticeable improvement in Form I-526E processing times compared to legacy EB-5 filings or direct EB-5 filings.
While timelines are not guaranteed, some post-RIA EB-5 investors, especially those associated with rural projects designated for priority processing, have received Form I-526E approvals in as little as 6 to 12 months. These observations are backward-looking and should not be interpreted as official USCIS benchmarks or predictive timelines. Processing times may be shorter or longer for any individual investor.
Key stages in the EB-5 process generally include:
Form I-526/I-526E Processing:
USCIS processing times vary and depend on multiple factors, including project structure, visa availability under the Department of State Visa Bulletin, USCIS adjudication capacity, and whether the investor is applying through consular processing or adjustment of status.
- Conditional residency: 2 years
- Form I-829 Processing: Form I-829 is filed to remove conditions on permanent residence near the end of the two-year conditional period. Processing times vary, and while some cases are adjudicated more quickly, others may take several years depending on case complexity, job creation evidence, and USCIS workload.
- Permanent residency: After USCIS approves Form I-829: Approval notification: 2-4 weeks after approval Receive permanent resident card (green card): 6-8 weeks after approval Receipt of physical green card: 8-12 weeks after approval
- Approval notification: 2-4 weeks after approval
- Receive permanent resident card (green card): 6-8 weeks after approval
- Receipt of physical green card: 8-12 weeks after approval
Factors influencing processing time include USCIS workload, the complexity of the application, background check delays, receiving an RFE (Request for Evidence), and interview scheduling. The timeline can also be affected by changes in law, policy, or adjudication practices.
There is no premium processing option for EB-5 investor petitions. Expedited processing may be requested only in limited circumstances and is granted solely at USCIS discretion. Working closely with experienced immigration counsel, submitting well-documented filings, and responding promptly to USCIS requests can help reduce avoidable delays.

### EB-5 Timeline: Summary of the Full Process
The EB-5 timeline, from initial preparation to unconditional permanent residency, spans several years. The following summary outlines each stage and the typical timeframe investors can expect:
- Pre-filing preparation (source of funds documentation, project selection, and investment): 1 to 6 months, depending on the complexity of the investor’s financial situation and the time needed to complete due diligence on the investment project.
- Form I-526 or I-526E filing and USCIS adjudication: Processing times vary. Some post-RIA I-526E filings associated with rural projects have received approvals in as little as 6 to 12 months, though timelines depend on USCIS workload, project structure, and visa availability.
- Adjustment of status (Form I-485) or consular processing: For investors already in the U.S. with an available visa number, adjustment of status may be filed concurrently with the I-526E petition. Consular processing timelines vary by country and consulate workload. Both pathways result in a conditional green card.
- Conditional residency period: 2 years from the date the conditional green card is issued.
- Form I-829 filing and USCIS review: Filed near the end of the two-year conditional period to remove conditions on permanent residence. Processing times vary and may take several years depending on case complexity and USCIS workload.
- Unconditional permanent residency: After USCIS approves Form I-829, the investor receives a 10-year green card with no conditions attached.
- Eligibility for U.S. citizenship: 5 years after receiving permanent residency, investors may apply for naturalization if they meet all requirements.
The total EB-5 timeline from initial filing to unconditional permanent residency typically ranges from approximately 3 to over 5 years, though individual timelines vary based on processing conditions, country of chargeability, and case-specific factors.
For a detailed comparison of EB-5 with other investment-based immigration options, see our guide on E-2 vs. EB-5: Comparing U.S. Investment Options .

## EB-5 Program Updates: Latest News and Policy Changes
We are committed to keeping our clients informed of the latest updates to EB-5 visa policy. These updates include the EB-5 Reform and Integrity Act of 2022 (RIA). This law established the EB-5 Integrity Fund, requiring regional centers to pay an annual fee. It also introduced changes to the regional center program, including new eligibility requirements and oversight measures.
Most importantly, the RIA allows for concurrent filing. Concurrently filing Form I-526 (Immigrant Petition by Standalone Investor) with Form I-485 (Application to Register Permanent Residence or Adjust Status, changing from temporary visa to green card while in the U.S.) allows EB-5 immigrant investors already in the United States to apply to change from nonimmigrant status to conditional permanent resident status. This allows investors to change their immigration status to “pending” and apply for an employment authorization document (EAD). In addition, after filing both forms, an investor can also apply for a travel permit. Investors can take advantage of concurrent filing to legally remain in the United States, travel freely in and out of the country, and work in the U.S.
Additional recent updates and policy changes to the EB-5 program include:
Regional Center Program authorization: Immigrant visas under the EB-5 Regional Center Program are authorized through Sept. 30, 2026.
Green card validity: In January 2023, USCIS extended the validity of Permanent Resident Cards for conditional permanent residents with pending Form I-751 or Form I-829 petitions to 48 months beyond the card’s expiration date
Visa availability approach : USCIS prioritizes Form I-526 petitions based on visa availability, with investors having an available visa or a visa soon to be available receiving priority processing

## FAQs on the EB-5 Visa Process
No, you must select the project yourself. However, your attorney can provide guidance on investment options, explain project requirements, and evaluate project risks to help you make an informed decision.
No, the law does not require you to invest the entire amount at once. However, you must demonstrate commitment to investing the full required amount and that the capital is “at risk” in the qualifying commercial enterprise. The timing and structure depend on your project agreement. We frequently work with business owners, often E-2 Treaty Investors, who started their businesses years ago and slowly reinvested profits into their business, such that they now meet the required investment threshold necessary to apply for an EB-5. Since they have already met the investment requirement, these business owners seek to apply for an EB-5, rather than pursue another immigration pathway.
Yes. Your spouse and unmarried children under 21 can receive derivative green cards and employment authorization. They can work for any employer anywhere in the U.S. without restrictions once they become lawful permanent residents or obtain valid work authorization while an adjustment application is pending.
No. You can live anywhere in the United States regardless of where your EB-5 investment project is located. The EB-5 visa does not restrict your residence to a specific geographic area as long as you maintain lawful permanent resident status.
Conditional residency lasts for 2 years and requires filing Form I-829 (Petition by Investor to Remove Conditions on Permanent Resident Status) before expiration. Permanent residency provides a 10-year green card with no conditions attached. Both allow you to live and work anywhere in the U.S.
Yes. In fact, Direct EB-5 investment often involves investing in your own new or existing U.S. business, as long as it qualifies and meets EB-5 requirements. Once you receive your conditional green card or valid employment authorization document (EAD) while your adjustment of status application is pending, you can also start any business anywhere in the U.S. The EB-5 visa does not restrict your employment or business activities.
Source of funds documentation proves your investment capital was obtained legally. You must provide evidence showing how you earned or acquired the money, such as tax returns, business records, property sale documents, inheritance records, or gift documentation, and must demonstrate a lawful path of funds from the original source to the EB-5 investment.
Yes, if you have an approved Advance Parole document (Form I-131). If you file concurrently, you can apply for Advance Parole at the same time and may receive it within several months, though processing times vary. Do not travel without approved Advance Parole, as it may be considered abandonment of your application if you are applying for adjustment of status in the U.S.

## Our EB-5 Visa Lawyers Can Help
Our EB-5 immigration attorneys guide investors through every step of the process, from selecting the right investment to obtaining permanent residency. With over 10,000 successful visa approvals, we help you avoid common pitfalls and maximize your chances of approval. Whether you’re considering rural priority processing or need guidance on source of funds documentation, our team provides the expertise you need to navigate the EB-5 process confidently.
